channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: After years of firm support, 10 days upended the US approach to Ukraine
item.description: As Ukraine approached the three-year mark of Russia’s full-scale invasion, the country’s hoped-for path to a favorable and lasting peace has been upended in a matter of days by the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fired employees fear beloved Yosemite National Park will lose its luster
item.description: Yosemite National Park employees who recently were fired by the U.S. government worry cuts will affect the experience of visitors and the welfare of wildlife that thrive in the popular vacation destination located in California’s Sierra Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Haven Daley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOJ issues complaint about federal judge’s ‘misconduct’ while presiding over military trans ban court case
item.description: The Department of Justice issued a complaint about the alleged "misconduct" of U.S. District Judge Ana Reyes while presiding over a case about trans people's service in the military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House and Ukraine nearing rare earths deal that would tighten relationship, AP source says
item.description: The White House and Ukraine have made significant progress toward reaching an agreement that would provide the U.S. with access to Ukraine’s rare earth minerals and tighten the long-term relationship between Kyiv and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: New FBI director Kash Patel plans to relocate up to 1,500 employees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel has told senior officials that he plans to relocate up to 1,000 employees from Washington to field offices around the country and move an additional 500 to a bureau facility in Huntsville, Alabama, according to a person with knowledge of the discussions.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge clears way for Trump administration to pull thousands of USAID staffers off the job
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cleared the way for one of the Trump administration's remaining steps in its dismantling of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge grants 19 AGs preliminary injunction against DOGE access to Treasury payment system
item.description: A federal judge on Friday further prevented the Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE, from accessing Treasury Department payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California lawmakers advocate for dual language immersion programs with new bill
item.description: Assemblymember Mark González is introducing AB 865, a bill that would grant the Dual Language Immersion program $5 million to increase access to instructional materials in partner languages, with the goal of educational equity and multilingual proficiency.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump’s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A federal judge has largely blocked sweeping executive orders from President Donald Trump that sought to end government support for programs promoting diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Maine female athlete 'grateful' for Trump's focus on trans competitors after local leaders 'failed' girls
item.description: A female high school track and field athlete is praising President Donald Trump for doing what she said Maine lawmakers won't: protect women from biological males competing against them in sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: ‘In Trump We Trust’ Is Exploding
item.description: President Trump forced through his Cabinet – we told you that would only increase his control over the Republican Party, and it did. To that point, Matt Lewis headlines in The Hill, “Conservatism used to mean something. Now it’s just whatever Trump says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Trump measure ignores ‘rising crime’ and ‘cost of living,’ blue state GOP officials charge
item.description: Democrats in the Colorado House of Representatives considered a resolution Friday that would condemn President Donald Trump's Jan. 6 pardons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department files complaint against judge weighing challenge to Trump’s transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department filed a complaint Friday accusing a federal judge in Washington of misconduct during hearings over President Donald Trump's executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel sworn in as ninth FBI director
item.description: Kash Patel was sworn in as FBI director Friday after a rocky path to confirmation and a narrow Senate vote Thursday. President Trump appointed him to end so-called "weaponization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP sues over White House access restrictions
item.description: The Associated Press sued White House officials Friday for restricting the wire service’s access to the Oval Office and Air Force One over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its AP Stylebook.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'thinking about' absorbing Postal Service into administration
item.description: President Trump said Friday he was considering merging the U.S. Postal Service into his administration, calling the agency a “tremendous loser” of money.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin ousts top ICE official over concerns about pace of illegal immigrant deportations
item.description: The Trump administration is replacing the head of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), over concerns over the pace of the deportation campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ATF chief legal counsel fired by Bondi in latest Justice Department shakeup
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi fired the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco and Firearms' chief legal counsel on Thursday, the former ATF official said on LinkedIn.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NIH funding cuts: Federal judge extends restraining order blocking Trump administration’s action
item.description: A Biden-appointed judge extended a temporary restraining order against the Trump administration’s cuts to National Institutes of Health research funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following a press conference with other Proud Boys members and Oath Keepers’ founder Stewart Rhodes.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to withhold federal funds from Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: In a statement, Maine Gov. Janet Mills, a Democrat, said the state “will not be intimidated” by Trump’s threat to withhold its federal funds.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court deals a severe blow to Holocaust survivors' lawsuit against Hungary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A unanimous Supreme Court on Friday dealt a severe blow to Holocaust survivors and their families in a long-running lawsuit seeking compensation from Hungary for property confiscated during World War II.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Ellison: Trump order on transgender athletes violates Minnesota law
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison’s opinion comes days after the Trump administration said it would investigate the state high school league’s plan to follow state law and let students participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Fellow senators have clapped for Mitch McConnell after he announced he won’t seek reelection next year. The Kentucky Republican is ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: A gold-framed Trump mug shot is hanging just outside the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump’s mug shot is one of the first things visitors will see when approaching the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul, MTA vow to fight President Trump over congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The MTA is suing the Trump administration after it announced plans to scrap New York City’s congestion pricing toll on Wednesday. A letter sent from U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy to Gov.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
